arts.ttl: DANNY MURPHY: Bukayo Saka is a star for England but his absence against Finland gives Lee Carsley the chance to deploy Phil Foden or Cole Palmer out wide and go back to basics
arts.sum: DANNY MURPHY: At 5ft 8in Saka isn't the biggest figure, but he has such wonderful strength on the ball under pressure and a knack of being able to roll defenders and win free-kicks.

arts.ttl: Jordan Pickford shares stance on England's future under Lee Carsley
arts.sum: Jordan Pickford has admitted that the decision on who will lead England permanently after Gareth Southgate is out of his hands, but hopes that his side can recover after their loss to Greece. He was at the helm when England were defeated 2-1 by Greece on Thursday and will also be in the dugout for their clash with Finland on Sunday.

arts.ttl: 5 talking points as England look to respond to Greece defeat against Finland
arts.sum: Helsinki is the next Nations League stop for Lee Carsley’s men.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's 'next Kaka', 14, tipped for England career after record-breaking goal
arts.sum: Arsenal wonderkid Max Dowman has received rave reviews following his performances in the Gunners' academy and has already been tipped to become a full England international

arts.ttl: Topi Keskinen: The Finland star who sports a tattoo of Wayne Rooney fishing
arts.sum: The Aberdeen winger said he had combined his ‘two favourite things in one tattoo’.

Two of the three most-bought players of Gameweek 8 have suffered fitness scares The post Saka injury latest + Johnson’s “precautionary” sub appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: England midfielder ranks Bukayo Saka, Cole Palmer & Phil Foden
arts.sum: Kalvin Phillips was put in the unenviable position of ranking three of England's brightest attacking talents; Arsenal's Bukayo Saka, Manchester City playmaker Phil Foden and Chelsea's talisman, Cole Palmer. The Ipswich Town midfielder has played with all three forwards, lining up for the England national team alongside Saka while spending extensive time on the training pitch with Foden and Palmer during the trio's shared time together at Manchester City.
